title: अन्नपूर्णा अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अन्नपूर्णा अष्टकम् ॥

ॐ नमो भगवत्यै अन्नपूर्णायै॥

नित्यानन्दकरी वराभयकरी सौन्दर्यरत्नाकरी
निर्धूताखिलघोरपावनकरी प्रत्यक्षमाहेश्वरी।
प्रालेयाचलवंशपावनकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥१॥

नानारत्नविचित्रभूषणकरी हेमाम्बराडम्बरी
मुक्ताहारविलम्बमानविलसद्वक्षोजकुम्भान्तरी।
काश्मीरागरुवासिताङ्गरुचिरे काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥२॥

योगानन्दकरी रिपुक्षयकरी धर्मार्थनिष्ठाकरी
चन्द्रार्कानलभासमानलहरी त्रैलोक्यरक्षाकरी।
सर्वैश्वर्यसमस्तवाञ्छितकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥३॥

कैलासाचलकन्दरालयकरी गौरी उमा शाङ्करी
कौमारी निगमार्थगोचरकरी ओंकारबीजाक्षरी।
मोक्षद्वारकपाटपाटनकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥४॥

दृश्याद्दृश्यविभूतिवाहनकरी ब्रह्माण्डभाण्डोदरी
लीलानाटकसूत्रभेदनकरी विज्ञानदीपाङ्कुरी।
श्रीविश्वेशमनः प्रसादनकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥५॥

उर्वीसर्वजनेश्वरी भगवती मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी
वेणीनीलसमानकुन्तलधरी नित्यान्नदानेश्वरी।
सर्वानन्दकरी सदा शुभकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥६॥

आदिक्षान्तसमस्तवर्णनकरी शम्भोस्त्रिभावाकरी
काश्मीरात्रिजलेश्वरी त्रिलहरी नित्यांकुरा शर्वरी।
कामाकाङ्क्षकरी जनोदयकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥७॥

देवी सर्वविचित्ररत्नरचिता दाक्षायणी सुन्दरी
वामे स्वादुपयोधरा प्रियकरी सौभाग्यमाहेश्वरी।
भक्ताभीष्टकरी सदा शुभकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी मातान्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥८॥

अन्नपूर्णे सदापूर्णे शंकरप्राणवल्लभे।
ज्ञानवैराग्यसिद्ध्यर्थं भिक्षां देहि च पार्वति॥

माता च पार्वती देवी पिता देवो महेश्वरः।
बान्धवाः शिवभक्ताश्च स्वदेशो भुवनत्रयम्॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितं अन्नपूर्णा अष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Annapurna Ashtakam ||

Om Namo Bhagavatyai Annapurnayai.

Nityananda-kari Vara-abhaya-kari Saundarya-ratnakari
Nirdhuta-akhila-ghora-pavana-kari Pratyaksha-maheshvari.
Praleyachala-vamsha-pavana-kari Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||1||

Nana-ratna-vichitra-bhushana-kari Hemambara-adambari
Mukta-hara-vilambamana-vilasad-vakshoja-kumbhantari.
Kashmira-agaru-vasita-anga-ruchire Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||2||

Yogananda-kari Ripu-kshaya-kari Dharmartha-nishtha-kari
Chandra-arka-anala-bhasamana-lahari Trailokya-raksha-kari.
Sarvaishvarya-samasta-vanchita-kari Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||3||

Kailasa-achala-kandaralaya-kari Gauri Uma Shankari
Kaumari Nigamartha-gochara-kari Omkarabija-akshari.
Moksha-dvara-kapata-patana-kari Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||4||

Drishyad Drishya-vibhuti-vahana-kari Brahmanda-bhandodari
Lila-nataka-sutra-bhedana-kari Vijnana-dipankuri.
Shri Vishvesha-manah Prasadana-kari Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||5||

Urvi-sarva-janeshvari Bhagavati Mata-annapurneshvari
Veni-nila-samana-kuntala-dhari Nitya-anna-daneshvari.
Sarvananda-kari Sada Shubha-kari Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||6||

Adi-kshanta-samasta-varnana-kari Shambhos Tri-bhavakari
Kashmira-atri-jaleshvari Tri-lahari Nityankura Sharvari.
Kama-akanksha-kari Janodaya-kari Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||7||

Devi Sarva-vichitra-ratna-rachita Dakshayani Sundari
Vame Svadu-payodhara Priya-kari Saubhagya-maheshvari.
Bhakta-abhishta-kari Sada Shubha-kari Kashi-puradhishvari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata-annapurneshvari.||8||

Annapurne Sadapurne Shankara-prana-vallabhe.
Jnana-vairagya-siddhyartham Bhiksham Dehi Cha Parvati.

Mata Cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshvarah.
Bandhavah Shiva-bhaktash Cha Svadesho Bhuvana-trayam.

|| Iti Shrimach-Shankaracharya-virachitam Annapurna Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
नित्यानन्दकारी, वर-अभयकारी, सौन्दर्यरत्नाकारी, समस्त घोरता का शमन करने वाली, प्रत्यक्ष माहेश्वरी, हिमालयवंश को पवित्र करने वाली काशीपुराधीश्वरी! हे कृपा का अवलम्बन करने वाली माता अन्नपूर्णेश्वरी! भिक्षा दो।

नाना रत्नों से विचित्र भूषण वाली, स्वर्णवस्त्रा, मुक्ताहारधारिणी, कश्मीर-अगरु से वासित अंगवाली काशीपुराधीश्वरी! भिक्षा दो।

योगानन्दकारी, शत्रुक्षयकारी, धर्मार्थनिष्ठाकारी, चन्द्र-सूर्य-अग्नि जैसी द्युतिवाली, त्रैलोक्यरक्षाकारी, सर्वैश्वर्य देने वाली! भिक्षा दो।

कैलास की कन्दरा में वास करने वाली, गौरी, उमा, शांकरी, कौमारी, ओंकारबीजाक्षरी, मोक्षद्वार खोलने वाली! भिक्षा दो।

ब्रह्माण्ड को उदर में धारण करने वाली, लीलानाटक का सूत्र छेदने वाली, विज्ञानदीपांकुरी! भिक्षा दो।

पृथ्वी की सर्वजनेश्वरी, नित्यान्नदानेश्वरी, सर्वानन्दकारी! भिक्षा दो।

हे अन्नपूर्णे! सदापूर्णे! शंकरप्राणवल्लभे! ज्ञान और वैराग्य की सिद्धि के लिए भिक्षा दो, हे पार्वती।

माता पार्वती हैं, पिता महेश्वर हैं, बन्धु शिवभक्त हैं और स्वदेश तीनों लोक हैं।
benefits: Composed by Adi Shankaracharya, one of the most celebrated ashtakams
Invokes Annapurna Devi of Kashi for food, nourishment, and spiritual sustenance
Invokes jnana (knowledge) and vairagya (detachment) along with material needs
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, hunger, and all forms of scarcity
Opens the door to moksha (liberation) through divine grace
Each verse culminates in the powerful prayer 'Bhiksham Dehi' (give alms)
description: Annapurna Ashtakam — अन्नपूर्णा अष्टकम् — is an eight-verse devotional hymn offered to Goddess Parvati. Rooted in Adi Shankaracharya, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to grant jnana (knowledge) and vairagya (detachment) along with material needs, composed by Adi Shankaracharya, one of the most celebrated ashtakams, and invoke Annapurna Devi of Kashi for food, nourishment, and spiritual sustenance. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Annapurna Jayanti. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Annapurna Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Annapurna Ashtakam by Adi Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses to Goddess Annapurna of Kashi for food, knowledge, and moksha.
relatedSearches: Annapurna Ashtakam lyrics in Sanskrit Hindi Tamil PDF
Annapurna Devi Kashi Vishwanath Varanasi temple
Annapurna Jayanti Margashirsha Purnima date significance
Mata Cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshwara verse
Annapurna mantra for food never to be scarce
Annapurna Devi iconography rice ladle pot
Annapurna puja vidhi for kitchen prosperity

seoFaqs.question: What is the Annapurna Ashtakam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Annapurna Ashtakam (ashta = eight, ka = verses) is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn attributed by tradition to Adi Shankaracharya (8th century CE), composed in praise of Goddess Annapurna, the form of Parvati who is the giver of food (anna = food, purna = full) and the presiding goddess of Kashi (Varanasi). Each verse begins with the Sanskrit phrase Nityanandakari Varabhayakari (giver of eternal bliss, boon and fearlessness) and concludes with the famous refrain Bhiksham Dehi Kripavalambanakari Mata Annapurneshwari — Grant me alms, O compassion-bestowing Mother Annapurneshwari. The hymn closes with the celebrated verse Mata Cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshwara, declaring Parvati as the universal mother. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya granthavali; Kashi Annapurna temple liturgy; Hindupedia Annapurna.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Annapurna and what is her significance?
seoFaqs.answer: Annapurna is the form of Parvati who governs nourishment, abundance and the kitchen — literally the goddess full of food (anna-purna), the cosmic feeder who ensures no being in the universe goes hungry. She is the queen of Kashi (Varanasi), where she shares the temple complex with Vishwanath (Shiva), and tradition holds that even Shiva accepts bhiksha (alms) from her hands as a kapalika. The Annapurna Devi temple at Kashi is one of the most revered shrines for kitchen-blessings, agricultural prosperity and freedom from hunger. Her iconography shows her seated on a throne holding a golden ladle (darvi) and a vessel of cooked rice (anna-patra). (Sources: Kashi Khanda of Skanda Purana; Devi Bhagavatam; Hindupedia Annapurna.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the story behind Adi Shankara composing the Annapurna Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition recounts that during Adi Shankara's pilgrimage to Kashi he witnessed the suffering of a starving family and was moved to invoke the Mother for the universal abolition of hunger. Standing before the Annapurna shrine adjacent to Kashi Vishwanath, he is said to have spontaneously composed this eight-verse hymn pleading bhiksham dehi (give me alms) — not for himself but as the cosmic plea of every hungry being. Mother Annapurna is said to have responded by appearing before him and granting that those who recite the hymn with devotion shall never lack food in their homes. The hymn became one of the most chanted before-meal prayers in the Smarta tradition. (Sources: Shankara Digvijaya of Madhava; Kashi Khanda; Hindupedia Adi Shankara.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Annapurna Ashtakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is most powerfully recited on Annapurna Jayanti (Margashirsha Purnima, December full moon), Akshaya Tritiya (Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya — the never-decreasing day, ideal for food and grain donations), every Friday (Lakshmi-Annapurna day), the entire Margashirsha and Kartik months, and during Navaratri. Daily recitation before cooking the morning meal, before consuming food (annam Brahma), and during annadana (food donation) ceremonies is the Smarta tradition. The verses are traditionally chanted facing east in the kitchen with offerings of rice, ghee and the first portion of cooked food (naivedya) before the family eats. (Sources: Drikpanchang Annapurna Jayanti; Smarta sandhya texts; Hindupedia Annadana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Mata Cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshwara?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing verse of the Annapurna Ashtakam — Mata Cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshwara, Bandhavah Shiva Bhaktashcha Svadesho Bhuvanatrayam — declares: My mother is Goddess Parvati, my father is Lord Maheshwara (Shiva), my kinsmen are the devotees of Shiva, and my native land is the three worlds. This verse, often recited independently as a daily affirmation, expresses the highest non-dual surrender in which the seeker abandons biological identity and adopts the cosmic family of Shiva-Parvati. It is one of the most quoted single verses in all of Adi Shankara's stotra literature and is often inscribed at the entrance to Hindu homes and temples. (Sources: Annapurna Ashtakam verse 8; Adi Shankaracharya granthavali; Smarta tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Annapurna at Kashi Vishwanath temple?
seoFaqs.answer: Kashi (Varanasi) is regarded as Annapurna's city — tradition holds that no one in Kashi shall die of hunger so long as the Mother resides there. The Annapurna Devi temple stands directly adjacent to the Kashi Vishwanath jyotirlinga, and the iconography of Shiva accepting alms from Annapurna's golden ladle is the central image of Kashi's spiritual economy: even the Lord depends on the Mother for sustenance. The temple serves free anna-prasada (food offering) daily, and devotees offer the first morsel of every meal mentally to Annapurna before eating. The annakuta (food-mountain) festival on Annapurna Jayanti sees a massive offering of cooked grains, sweets and prepared dishes piled before the goddess. (Sources: Kashi Khanda of Skanda Purana; Kashi Vishwanath temple records; Hindupedia Kashi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Annapurna Ashtakam promise to its reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that regular recitation grants freedom from poverty and hunger (kshudha-nivritti) for the household and seven generations, abundance of grain and food in the kitchen pantry, success in food-related professions (cooks, restaurateurs, farmers, grain-traders), the power to feed unexpected guests without shortage, recovery of digestive health and removal of food-related diseases, blessings for fertility and childbirth (Annapurna being the cosmic feeding mother), and the spiritual fruit of vairagya (detachment) and jnana (knowledge) — for the verses culminate in the recognition of Brahman as the cosmic anna. The Smarta tradition holds that one who recites this hymn before meals never accrues the karma of stolen or impure food. (Sources: Annapurna Ashtakam phalashruti; Kashi Khanda; Hindupedia Annapurna upasana.)
